title: Something Lives in the Hollow Oak

pageTimings.text:
It was late in the Hollow Oak.
Everyone was asleep.
Then Pip heard it.
Scratch.
Shuffle.
A tiny voice: "...where DID I put them..."

Pip woke Wren.
They listened together.
Scratch.
Shuffle.
"...they were right here..."
"Something is in the walls," said Wren.

By morning, everyone knew.
They stood outside the Hollow Oak.
"It makes those sounds," said Luna.
"It always has."
"It HAS?" said everyone.

Pip, Wren, Burrow, and Fern went inside to search.
They searched every room.
They found nothing.
Then Burrow's nose twitched.

"There is a room here," said Burrow.
He found a small door near the roots.
It was very small.
Too small for most of them.
"I can go," said Fern.

Fern went through the small door.
She was inside for a moment.
Then she called back:
"There is someone here."
Everyone pressed their ears to the door.

Inside was a room.
It was small and warm.
It was full of collected things.
A tiny creature was searching through them.
She did not look up.

"Hello," said Fern.
The tiny creature looked up.
"Have you seen my spectacles?" she said.
"I've been looking for three days."

Fern looked around the room.
Then she looked at Twig.
The spectacles were on top of Twig's head.
They had been there all along.

"I am Twig," said the tiny creature.
"I live here."
"I have always lived here."
She seemed perfectly happy about this.

Fern came back through the door.
"Her name is Twig," said Fern.
"She has lived here for many years."
Everyone looked at Luna.
"Of course," said Luna.
"Twig."

They made Twig a slightly larger door.
Twig came out for acorn biscuits.
She sat in the big room and looked around.
"I like it here," she said.
"But I like my room better."
"This is also very nice."
